result.summary: Checkout p99 latency jumped 12× starting 14:05 UTC; throughput and error rate barely moved, so this is a per-request slowdown, not an outage.
Logs show a burst of identical slow per-line-item SELECTs during cart serialization — the signature of an N+1 query, not lock contention or timeouts.
One deploy lands inside the onset window: checkout-svc v2.31, a cart-serialization refactor. Nothing else changed in the 2h before 14:05.

result.hypothesis: v2.31 replaced one batched JOIN with a per-line-item lookup — a classic N+1
result.mechanism: cart.rb#serialize now maps over line_items and calls LineItem.find per row instead of eager-loading; a 40-item cart fires 40 queries

result.proof: On the held repro, forcing the eager-loaded JOIN path drops p99 from 2.8s to 260ms; query count per checkout falls from 41 to 2.
result.residual_risk: low — the JOIN was the pre-v2.31 behavior, already load-tested

result.recommended_direction: Restore the batched eager-load in cart.rb and lock it in with an N+1 regression test
result.diff_summary: cart.rb:142 — replace `line_items.map { |li| LineItem.find(li.id) }` with `line_items.includes(:variant, :promotions)`; revert the serializer to use the preloaded association
tests_to_add: spec/requests/cart_n_plus_one_spec.rb — assert ≤ 2 queries per cart serialize
